steps.title: Identify the second verb and its tense
steps.content: The relative clause contains two predicates sharing the subject whose work: one describing the richness of Betham's poems, and encourages, which is in simple present tense.

steps.title: Ensure parallel verb forms in the compound predicate
steps.content:
Highlights uses simple present tense, matching encourages and creating a parallel compound predicate where both verbs share the same subject.

Has highlighted (perfect tense), is highlighting (progressive tense), and highlighting (non-finite form) all mismatch with encourages.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
